description_long: This is a two-story rectangular motel building with a flat roof and a prominent overhanging awning along the front facade. The exterior is finished in light-colored concrete or stucco, with a row of guest room doors and windows facing the parking lot. A large vertical sign reading 'MOTEL' is mounted near the front entrance, and there are two vehicles parked directly in front of the building, emphasizing its roadside accessibility. The front entrance is accessed by a set of stairs leading up to a covered walkway, and there are several utility elements such as trash bins and an antenna visible on the roof and side. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on function, typical of mid-20th-century American motels.
description: A two-story, flat-roofed motel with a light exterior, front-facing guest rooms, a large 'MOTEL' sign, and adjacent parking. The design is simple and functional, with exterior corridor access and visible utility features.
description_view_0: Side view showing the building's length, roof antenna, and two parked vehicles in front of the motel.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the main entrance, large 'MOTEL' sign, covered walkway, and parking lot directly in front.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the stairs to the upper level, blue trash bins, and the side of the parking lot.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the plain back wall, utility access ladder, and trash bins, with the 'MOTEL' sign visible at the corner.
